metric_value: IA Continuum: expand single architecture from smartphones to datacenters
notes: Central thesis of the paper. Intel using IA DNA to create application-specific processor variants.

metric_value: Technology-leading market creator; invests to build ecosystems before products ship
notes: Intel metamorphosed from technology leader to technology-leading market creator over the past decade.

metric_value: Hundreds of millions invested in WiFi infrastructure 2003-2005 to drive notebook adoption
notes: Intel invested to standardize WiFi infrastructure, software, and standards across businesses and carriers globally.

metric_value: Bootstrapped global Linux-based CE software platform (Moblin) to replace Windows in CE devices
notes: Intel rallied Linux community around Moblin, invested millions in developer training and app store.

metric_value: Two-year cycle alternating new microarchitecture (tock) and die shrink (tick)
notes: Paper describes tick-tock as Intel's consistent delivery process for silicon products.

metric_value: ~1 billion processors/year approaching, projected >2 billion by end of decade
notes: Total addressable market for silicon processors. Desktop microprocessors ~150 million of that total.

metric_value: ~60% of PC market
notes: Notebooks approaching 60% of total PC sales units in 2010.

metric_value: ~10 million processors/year, expected to more than double this decade
notes: Server chip run-rate in 2010 with high margins making it highly attractive market.

metric_value: ~10% of cell phones are smartphones; projected to grow to ~90% by end of decade
notes: Massive growth opportunity identified in smartphone market.

metric_value: ~1 billion CE units/year by mid-decade
notes: Consumer electronics market explosion driven by Internet connectivity in every device.

metric_value: 34 gigabytes/day (US)
notes: Cited from University of California report. 3.6 zettabytes total for US in 2008.

metric_value: ~33% of Intel business by units; channel represents the bulk and growing faster
notes: DAHL (Dell, Acer, HP, Lenovo) represent only about a third; channel is the bulk of Intel business.

metric_value: 35 million units growing to 60 million by late decade
notes: Automotive embedded processor opportunity for Intel SOCs.

metric_value: Moorestown good enough for a few smartphone design wins; Medfield next year will reach sub-watt levels
notes: Paper assesses Atom as not yet fully competitive in smartphones but on trajectory.

metric_value: Far from a write-off; redirected from discrete graphics to HPC with new visualization instructions
notes: Author concludes Larrabee can become significant Intel business despite critics. Has multiple IA cores plus visualization primitives.

metric_value: 48-core prototype demonstrated Dec 2009; each core runs own virtual OS with TCP/IP; could be productized within a year
notes: Terascale R&D testbed for many simple IA cores on a chip. Useful for FPGA replacement and cloud.

metric_value: Not IA-compatible but RAS features increasingly migrated to Xeon; shares I/O chipset with Nehalem-EX
notes: Itanium DNA indirectly improves all IA hardware through RAS feature migration.

metric_value: Reviewers uniformly impressed; integrated graphics suitable for many games and HDTV-quality output
notes: Westmere 32nm tick introduced Clarkdale (desktop) and Arrandale (notebook) with integrated graphics, Turbo Boost.

metric_value: Fortune 500 convinced of TCO savings; next target is global SMB market
notes: vPro enterprise management and security platform embedded in PC refresh cycles.

metric_value: Intel investing to lead in enterprise/consumer SSDs; millions of drives expected as costs drop; rapid datacenter adoption by mid-decade
notes: Intel makes SSD controller; SSDs several times faster than hard drives for datacenter reads.

metric_value: Big hype trend with hazy definitions but real merit; Intel extending Xeon up (more cores) and down (low-wattage) for cloud
notes: Cloud architecture anticipates massive, variable workloads requiring scalable server infrastructure.

metric_value: Small piece in next couple years but could be major player by mid-decade with sustained investment
notes: Author hedges on near-term but sees long-term smartphone opportunity for Intel.

metric_value: Billion dollar SOC market share for Intel towards end of decade
notes: Embedded SOC market has glacial design cycles but long revenue streams.

metric_value: IA fits market requirements of vast majority of decade's computing work requirements
notes: Author's core conclusion: IA singularly capable of spanning the computing continuum.

metric_value: Can become significant source of Intel business despite critics calling it a write-off
notes: Author sees Larrabee potential in HPC, visual computing (3D petroleum models), computer animation.

metric_value: By end of decade silicon <10nm; much less clear how Moore's Law will perform in 2020s
notes: Paper flags Moore's Law sustainability as an open question for the 2020s.

metric_value: Failed; Intel cancelled Atom mobile (Broxton/SoFIA) in 2016. ARM architecture dominated smartphones entirely.
notes: Intel's smartphone ambitions never materialized despite billions invested. Only a handful of Atom phones shipped (e.g., some Lenovo, ASUS models).

metric_value: Cancelled as discrete GPU product in 2010; technology evolved into Xeon Phi (Knights Corner 2012, Knights Landing 2016), then Xeon Phi discontinued 2020.
notes: Larrabee never shipped as product. Concept partially validated: GPU computing became massive market, but NVIDIA dominated with CUDA. Intel later launched Arc GPUs (2022).

metric_value: Failed; only Nokia N9 shipped with MeeGo. Nokia switched to Windows Phone (2011), then acquired by Microsoft (2014). MeeGo forked into Tizen and Sailfish OS.
notes: The Moblin/MeeGo Linux CE platform strategy did not achieve the scale Intel envisioned.

metric_value: Intel sold Wind River to TPG Capital in April 2018 after 9 years. Wind River later acquired by Aptiv in 2022.
notes: Wind River did not become the CE integration linchpin Intel envisioned. Continued as independent embedded OS company.

metric_value: Intel maintained ~90%+ server CPU market share through most of 2010s; AMD EPYC eroded share significantly from 2019 onward.
notes: Paper's datacenter predictions proved highly accurate. Server market more than doubled as predicted.

metric_value: SSDs became dominant datacenter storage by mid-decade as predicted. Intel was major player; sold NAND business to SK Hynix in 2021.
notes: SSD adoption trajectory prediction proved accurate. Intel eventually exited NAND manufacturing.

metric_value: Cloud computing became dominant IT paradigm; multi-hundred-billion dollar market by 2020. Far exceeded 2010 expectations.
notes: Paper identified cloud as emerging trend with 'hazy definitions' -- it became the defining computing paradigm of the decade.

metric_value: Smartphone penetration reached ~80% of mobile phones globally by 2020, approaching the predicted 90%.
notes: Paper's 10% to 90% smartphone growth prediction was directionally correct.

metric_value: Nokia mobile division acquired by Microsoft in 2014 for $7.2B; Microsoft wrote off the acquisition by 2015. Nokia pivoted to telecom infrastructure.
notes: Nokia was presented as key MeeGo partner; the partnership collapsed within a year of paper's publication.

metric_value: Only one technology company in the world could propose a common computing architecture from CE to datacenters: Intel
notes: Strong endorsement of Intel's unique position. Author cites technology tools, execution ability, and vision.

metric_value: Most difficult challenge is not hardware but software: parallel programming bottleneck and growing software driver workload
notes: Software parallelism remains a sticky problem; programmers slow to distribute applications across parallel hardware.

metric_value: Government regulatory action is distracting mosquito; Intel targeted because it is big and successful, like Microsoft, Apple, Google
notes: FTC antitrust suit filed after AMD settlement. Author frames as political rather than substantive.

metric_value: Embedded systems market ripe for Intel SOC approach; brings time-to-market, lifecycle cost, and development labor cost benefits
notes: Author convinced embedded SOC will yield growing and prolonged revenue streams later in decade.

metric_value: Windows Mobile produced mediocre results after a decade of development
notes: Author's assessment of Microsoft's mobile efforts, used to justify Intel's Linux/Moblin strategy.

metric_value: Core i7-980x: 20 GHz aggregate over 6 cores (12 threads); most powerful consumer chip ever at time
notes: High-end Westmere desktop processor.

metric_value: Over 400 of Top 500 Supercomputing Sites used Intel processors (Nov 2009)
notes: Demonstrates Intel's HPC dominance; 80%+ of Top 500 list.

metric_value: ~60% margins on microprocessor chips ($50-$3000 ASP range)
notes: Turning sand into $50-$3000 chips at 60% margins described as great business.

metric_value: Wireless connectivity, long battery life, intuitive UI, instant on-off, strong security, 6-month product development cycles
notes: Key CE product feature requirements identified for the 2010s decade.

metric_value: Power efficiency per watt, virtualization-driven consolidation, and cloud computing scalability
notes: Three forces driving datacenter refresh cycles over next five years.

metric_value: PCs Per Person (PPP) ratio increase in developed markets; middle class emergence in developing markets
notes: Two forces driving PC innovation: PPP in developed economies and emerging market middle class growth.

metric_value: Atom (CE/smartphones), SOC (embedded), Core i3/i5/i7 (desktops/notebooks), Xeon (servers/workstations), Xeon variants (storage/comms)
notes: The five segments of Intel's IA continuum product architecture.

metric_value: It's no longer about personal computers, it's about making computing personal -- multiple devices matching time, place, and needs
notes: Intel's tagline capturing the shift from PC-centric to person-centric computing.

metric_value: Itanium is #2 selling architecture for mission-critical workloads behind only IBM mainframe/Power
notes: Itanium positioned in mission-critical segment despite not being IA-compatible.

metric_value: Nokia MeeGo merged with Intel Moblin (Feb 2010); combined with Wind River acquisition (2009) to create CE software stack
notes: Three-pronged CE software strategy: Moblin open-source OS, Nokia MeeGo merger, and Wind River integration expertise.
